prompts.abstract: The object is a rectangular plate with a flat top and bottom surfaces. It has a rectangular shape with rounded corners and a small notch at the top center. The plate is symmetrical along its length.
prompts.description: The object is a rectangular plate with a flat top and bottom surfaces. It has a rectangular shape with rounded corners and a small notch at the top center. The plate is symmetrical along its length.
prompts.beginner: The design features a rectangular plate with a complex, multi-segmented outline. The plate is scaled down and transformed into a three-dimensional object, resulting in a solid rectangular shape measuring roughly 0.75 units long, 0.1585 units wide, and 0.0102 units thick.
prompts.intermediate: The design involves creating a rectangular plate with a series of interconnected faces. The first face forms a complex shape with multiple segments, connecting points along the edges to create a detailed outline. The second face mirrors this complexity, extending the structure vertically. The third and fourth faces add additional layers to the design, while the fifth face completes the top surface. These faces are carefully aligned to form a cohesive structure. After defining the two-dimensional sketch, it is scaled down by 75% and transformed into a three-dimensional object. The final step is to extrude the sketch to create a solid body. The resulting rectangular plate measures approximately 0.75 units in length, 0.1585 units in width, and 0.0102 units in height.
prompts.expert: Start by creating a new coordinate system with Euler angles set to zero degrees for all axes and a translation vector also set to zero. For the first face, draw a loop consisting of eight lines. Begin from the origin at (0.0, 0.0) and draw a line to (0.3644, 0.0), then proceed to (0.3644, 0.0095), (0.375, 0.0095), (0.375, 0.0793), (0.0095, 0.0793), (0.0095, 0.0687), (0.0, 0.0687), and finally back to the origin. For the second face, draw another loop with eight lines starting from (0.0, 0.0899) to (0.0095, 0.0899), (0.0095, 0.0793), (0.375, 0.0793), (0.375, 0.149), (0.3644, 0.149), (0.3644, 0.1585), (0.0, 0.1585), and back to (0.0, 0.0899). The third face consists of a loop with five lines: start at (0.3644, 0.0) and draw to (0.3856, 0.0), (0.3856, 0.0095), (0.375, 0.0095), and back to (0.3644, 0.0). For the fourth face, draw a loop with eight lines starting from (0.375, 0.0095) to (0.3856, 0.0095), (0.3856, 0.0), (0.75, 0.0), (0.75, 0.0687), (0.7405, 0.0687), (0.7405, 0.0793), (0.375, 0.0793), and back to (0.375, 0.0095). The fifth face has a loop with eight lines: start at (0.375, 0.0793) and draw to (0.7405, 0.0793), (0.7405, 0.0899), (0.75, 0.0899), (0.75, 0.1585), (0.3856, 0.1585), (0.3856, 0.149), (0.375, 0.149), and back to (0.375, 0.0793). After drawing all the faces, apply a scale factor of 0.75 to the two-dimensional sketch. Transform the scaled sketch into three dimensions by rotating it using Euler angles set to zero degrees and translating it using a vector of zeros. Finally, extrude the sketch 0.0102 units in the positive normal direction to create a new solid body. The final dimensions of the rectangular plate are 0.7500000000000004 units in length, 0.1585360158188455 units in width, and 0.010161999203976731 units in height.
